name: Shock-Marine
physical: Humans encased in bulky, mass-produced powered exosuits that hum with servo assistance. The exosuit is a crude framework of hydraulic pistons, armored plates, and sealed joint covers bolted over a pressurized bodyglove. The helmet is a full-face visor with a HUD visible as a faint green glow behind the faceplate. They carry belt-fed ballistic assault rifles with underslung equipment rails — the standard M7, a weapon so ubiquitous that every soldier can field-strip one blindfolded. Power cells visible as blocky protrusions on the lower back. When they fire, the rifle's report is a deafening supersonic CRACK, and physical brass casings eject in an arc.
narrative: Shock-Marines are the Terran League's answer to every tactical problem: put enough of them on the field and something will work. They are not elite. Not specialized. They are competent, trained, disciplined soldiers in reliable equipment. The galaxy will fear them not because any single Marine is extraordinary, but because the Marine NEXT to them, and the hundred behind, are all exactly as competent and unwilling to retreat. When a Shock-Marine dies, they leave behind a body in a dented exosuit, a rifle with a half-loaded magazine, and a small personal effect — a photo, a letter, a coin from a colony that may not exist anymore.
repair: PRIMARY beneficiary of Field Repair drones. Modular exosuits DESIGNED for drone-assisted repair — standardized connection points allow rapid patching. Effectively +2% HP regen/sec on top of drone healing because the suit assists the process.

name: Hellfire-Trooper
physical: Heavy infantry in bulky, heat-resistant hazard suits layered over exosuit frames. Adds 40% bulk — distinctive wide-shouldered, lumbering silhouette. Pressurized promethium tanks on back — cylindrical armored containers connected by reinforced fuel lines to a handheld ignition nozzle. The nozzle is a meter-long wand with a pilot flame at the tip. When firing: roaring liquid-fire arc — viscous orange-white chemical gel arcing 30 meters, sticking to any surface. Deep guttural WHOOSH followed by crackling roar. Air shimmers with heat distortion.
narrative: Specialists in the ugly art of clearance. When a bunker won't fall to explosives, when a trench network is too deep for artillery — the Hellfire-Troopers fill the defensive position with liquid fire at 1,200°C. The fire doesn't just kill — it denies terrain. A promethium-soaked trench is impassable for minutes. The smell is what veterans talk about. The AFTERMATH. Chemical stench of promethium mixed with scorched metal, burned earth. Hellfire-Troopers eat separately from other units. No one makes them. They just do.
repair: MORE DIFFICULT for drones to service due to hazardous chemical systems. Drones prioritize other units, only servicing Hellfire-Troopers when no other unit is below 75% HP.

name: Aegis-Walker
physical: Fast bipedal mech, four meters tall, glass canopy cockpit with single pilot in gyroscopic harness. Squat armored torso housing reactor and ammo stores. Two digitigrade legs with shock-absorbing knee joints — sprint, strafe, pivot with surprising agility. Twin-linked rotary cannons on articulated weapon arms — six-barreled gatling at 3,000 RPM per barrel. Shoulder-mounted missile pod: four guided missiles in box launcher. Pilot visible through canopy — jaw clenched, eyes on targeting displays, body vibrating with recoil.
narrative: The Terran League's cavalry — fast enough to exploit gaps, armed enough to punish anything caught, armored enough to survive the sprint. Pilots selected from the top 2% of combat vehicle operators, universally cheerfully insane. Operate in pairs, creating overlapping kill zones. The sound: rising whine of spin-up, tearing-fabric roar of sustained fire, brass-rain patter of thousands of casings hitting the ground.
repair: FAVORITE target for Field Repair drones. Cockpit canopy, weapon arms, and leg joints all have standardized drone access points. A limping Walker restored to full mobility in 15 seconds — faster than any other vehicle class.

name: Siege-Breaker Tank
physical: Blocky, low-profile battle tank on heavy composite treads. Layered reactive armor plates designed to detonate outward when struck. Squat angular turret housing 120mm smoothbore cannon with muzzle brake the size of a human torso. Firing sequence: hydraulic suspension anchors deploy, gripping ground. Half-second pause. Concussive BOOM — 1,800 m/s HEAT round downrange. Turret rocks. Spent casing ejects with mechanical CLANG. Loader manually feeds next round. Crew of four: commander, gunner, loader, driver.
narrative: The Terran League's armored fist. Where Marines hold and Walkers flank, the Siege-Breaker punches THROUGH. 120mm cannon defeats any armored target at combat range. Target doesn't just take damage — it JOLTS, physically displaced by impact. Tank crews develop a particular bond: four people sealed in a metal box, breathing recycled air, trusting each other with their lives. The loader's arms ache. The driver can't see. The gunner reduces life and death to crosshairs. The commander makes the call.
repair: Benefits HEAVILY from Field Repairs due to layered armor. When reactive plates detonate, drones replace them from stored spares in under 20 seconds — effectively restoring explosive reactive armor mid-battle. Exceptionally durable in sustained engagements.

name: Titan-Class Leviathan
physical: Towering multi-tracked mobile fortress. Six independent tread assemblies, each wider than a standard tank, in three pairs along a city-block hull. Superstructure rises four stories: twin heavy gun batteries (fore/aft), eight PD turret clusters, two missile arrays, roof-mounted command antenna. Armor so thick it creates meter-deep track marks. Internal: automated ammo factory, field hospital, deployable command post. When it moves, the ground trembles. Maximum speed: walking pace.
narrative: The Terran League's declaration of intent: we are here, we are staying, you cannot move us. Deploying a Leviathan is a STRATEGIC decision. It takes cycles to move, cycles to deploy, enormous Scrap to maintain. But once deployed, it transforms the battlefield. Infantry rally in its shadow. Tanks form escort columns. Drones operate at double efficiency within 200m. Killing one requires concentrated capital-grade firepower. Crew complement: 120 — a small community. Own mess hall, barracks, medical bay. They call it 'the inside.'
repair: Doesn't just benefit from Field Repairs — it PROVIDES them. Internal drone fabrication facility DOUBLES the repair drone pool for all Terran units within 200 meters. A mobile repair zone.

name: Wraith-Fighter
physical: Aerodynamic swept-wing superiority fighter with twin afterburner engines cracking the sound barrier. Angular, aggressive predator silhouette. Single-bubble reinforced glass canopy, 270° field of view. Underwing hardpoints: air-to-air missiles and guided bombs. At full afterburn: twin contrails of superheated exhaust, Mach 3 — the sound arrives AFTER the fighter has passed.
narrative: Wraith-Fighter pilots are the Terran League's aristocrats. They fight clean wars in climate-controlled cockpits above the mud and blood. At least, that's the reputation. The reality: pilots die alone in fireballs at 900 m/s, and the comfortable cockpit becomes a coffin when canopy seals won't release during emergency descent. In battle: establish air superiority, intercept bombers, then transition to precision ground support.
repair: Standard drone servicing upon return to carrier or airstrip.

name: Valkyrie Gunship
physical: Heavily armored twin-engine VTOL. Massive rotary fans in reinforced nacelles. Wider than long — stubby wings with rocket pod arrays, chin-mounted gatling cannons. Flying brick of titanium and ablative plating. Enters loud and heavy, fans screaming, kicking up dust storms. Continuous flare dispensers during approach.
narrative: When infantry are pinned and Siege-Breakers can't advance, the Valkyrie arrives. Drops into the combat zone hovering above treetops. Wing-mounted rocket pods fire in rippling salvos — walking destruction across the enemy position. Concussive force suppresses infantry in cover. Shrapnel does the rest. Crew of two: pilot and weapons officer in symbiotic relationship — one doesn't survive without the other.

name: Orbital-Strike Drone
physical: Sleek, unmanned wedge of radar-absorbent composite. No cockpit — sensor cluster in nose beneath faceted radome. Payload bay is 60% of internal volume: single massive bunker-buster with penetrator tip and delayed-detonation thermobaric fuse. Operational profile: climbs to atmosphere edge under jet power, shuts down all systems, enters unpowered Mach 6 vertical dive. No engine noise, no exhaust, no warning until the supersonic shock wave.
narrative: The Terran League's surgical answer to hardened targets. Arrives silently — the unpowered dive produces no warning until impact. The explosion is final. Column of debris, ground cradles, secondary explosions. When dust settles: a crater where the fortified position was. One drone per target. One warhead per drone. Expensive, irreplaceable. Each deployment is a strategic investment.

name: Condor Dropship
physical: Flying brick of titanium and ablative plating. Two massive turbofan engines bolted to sides. Rear-loading ramp deploys a full squad in four seconds. Intentionally aggressive descent at steep angles through anti-air fire — relies on thick armor, not agility. Cockpit armored against direct flak. Troop compartment padded, pressurized, crash-harness equipped.
narrative: The worst experience in the Terran League military. Troops call it 'the coffin drop' — sealed in a lightless metal box, no windows, pilot's voice crackling: 'Flak hit, port side, no breach. Hold on — evasive. Impact in five. Four. Three. Two —' The ramp drops. Daylight floods in. Marines sprint down into whatever hell awaits. Condor lifts off immediately. One-way ticket to the war zone. Extraction is someone else's problem.

name: Stratos-Bomber
physical: Massive high-altitude bomber. Wingspan twice the Wraith-Fighter. Payload bay runs entire fuselage length. Operates above most ground-based anti-air, appearing as distant silver cross. Four turbofan engines. Bomb bay doors open in sequence — dozens of unguided HE bombs cascading in calculated patterns. Bombs are heavy, cheap, numerous. Precision through volume.
narrative: The Terran League's oldest and most controversial weapon. Carpet bombing — deliberate, systematic destruction without discrimination. A single sortie turns a forest into a crater-pocked wasteland, reduces an urban district to rubble, denies an entire territory. After-action reports: 'Area denial achieved. Estimated hostile casualties: significant. Collateral damage: total.' The weapon of the Vanguard and the Tyrant. But when the Vorax swarm with thousands of Scythe-Fiends — suddenly doesn't feel controversial.

name: Sabre-Interceptor
physical: Rugged, heavily armored single-seat void fighter. Not aerodynamic — compact angular brick of armor plate with twin chemical thrusters and forward-mounted tungsten autocannons. Cramped pressurized cockpit capsule surrounded by centimeters of armor. Thick reinforced glass with anti-spalling coating. A pilot sealed inside a flying safe.
narrative: Sabre pilots survive where others die. Not the fastest, not the most agile, not the most comfortable — but built to TAKE HITS and keep fighting. Absorbs enemy fire on thick hull, returns fire with physical rounds that don't care about energy shields, limps back to carrier for repair. Sabre pilots have a saying: 'Glass jaw or iron chin.' They chose iron.

name: Aegis-Frigate
physical: Defensive ship bristling with automated flak cannons and PD laser turrets. Light hull — not designed for broadside exchanges. Every surface mount is a rapid-tracking weapon for small fast targets: fighters, torpedoes, boarding pods, Sporeshots. Central hull studded with dozens of rotating turret clusters, each independently tracking. Continuous eruption of fire — tracer rounds, flak bursts, PD beams creating a blender of tungsten shrapnel.
narrative: Sails ahead of capital ships, turning the void into a no-fly zone. Automated fire-control and human oversight track hundreds of targets simultaneously. A single Aegis-Frigate protects a capital ship from an entire fighter squadron. Two frigates make space impassable to anything smaller than a cruiser. Against capital ships: nearly useless. Relies on the fleet's capitals for offensive power. MVP armada: 2 Frigates at game start. HP persists across battles — they do not heal between engagements.
repair: Standard drone servicing. NOTE: In MVP, armada HP does NOT restore between battles. Destroyed frigates are permanently spliced from the fleet.

name: Broadsword-Cruiser
physical: Massive wedge-shaped capital ship in thick modular armor. Ship of the line — fights broadsides, trades damage, wins through durability. Macro-cannon batteries: rows of turrets along both broadsides, each housing a cannon loading apartment-sized explosive kinetic shells. Loading partially automated but requires human oversight — armored crews wrestling shells into breeches. Full broadside: entire ship shudders, staggered sequence, wall of kinetic projectiles, shells large enough to track with the naked eye.
narrative: Naval combat in the Terran tradition. Two Broadswords squaring off: brutal grinding — both firing broadsides, armor crumbling under impacts, modular plates shattering and being replaced. The winner: whose crew loads faster, whose repair crews seal breaches quicker, whose captain maintains discipline under sustained bombardment. Broadsword captains are steady, patient, and slightly deaf from years near macro-cannon mounts. Space combat as a test of will: whoever flinches first, dies.
repair: Standard drone servicing + internal repair systems.

name: Carrier-Command
physical: Sprawling mobile flight deck crawling with EVA mechanics and repair bays. Largest dedicated carrier. Dorsal flight deck — flat magnetized landing strip where Sabres launch and recover in continuous cycles. Below: hangar bay with reserve fighters, munitions, repair facilities. EVA mechanics in pressure suits work on hull during combat. Lightly armed for its size — PD clusters and single forward lance. Value is in fighters: sustains 40+ Sabre-Interceptors.
narrative: Holds back from the battle line, letting Broadswords and Frigates form the front. Launches wave after wave of manned interceptors — pilots pulling G-forces in coffin-sized cockpits, firing tungsten rounds while repair drones race to service returnees. Crew: 800+ personnel including pilots, mechanics, flight directors, munitions handlers, medical staff. A floating city dedicated to keeping fighters in the fight.
repair: PROVIDES repair infrastructure for entire fighter wing.

name: Nova-Dreadnought
physical: Colossal flagship built around dual-core fusion reactor. Kilometer-long wedge of layered armor, bristling with weapons. Signature weapon: spinal-mounted Magnetic Accelerator Cannon (MAC) — railgun running the ship's entire length. Fires depleted-uranium slugs at fractional lightspeed. Loading diverts ALL power for 3 seconds — lights dim, PD offline, engines to minimum. Electromagnetic discharge arcs along hull. Slug exits as streak of light. Impact snaps a capital ship in half.
narrative: The Terran League's final argument. Deployed when diplomacy has failed, combined arms have failed, every option exhausted. The MAC shot: the moment every space battle holds its breath. The Dreadnought aligns — slowly, ponderously, entire kilometer rotating. Everyone knows what's coming. 3-second power diversion countdown visible: lights dimming like a beast drawing breath. Then the shot. A streak. A kinetic impact in megatons. An enemy ship that was whole is now two pieces drifting apart. One shot. One kill. Then reload, and the fleet covers.
repair: Carries own dedicated repair drone squadron (separate from fleet pool). Self-repairs during combat. Must overwhelm repair capacity with sustained concentrated fire, or it simply heals between volleys.
